sections.paragraph:
ὀπτάω (ὀπτός) aor. ὤπτησα LXX; pass subj. 3 sg. ὀπτηθῇ (Ar.) (Hom. et al.; PSI 402, 5 [III b.c.]; PLond III, 131, 115 p. 173; LXX; Jos., Ant. 1, 197; 3, 255; Ar. 4, 3; Just.) bake (so Hdt. 8, 137; X., An. 5, 4, 29 ἄρτους ὀπτῶντες) MPol 15:2 ἄρτος ὀπτώμενος.—DELG s.v. ὀπτό.

aor. aor. = aorist

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

subj. subj. = subjunctive; subject

  1. sg. = singular

Ar. Ar. = Aristides, apologist, II a.d.—List 5

Hom Hom , VIII b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

PSI PSI = Papiri greci e latini: Pubblicazioni della Società Italiana—List 4

PLond PLond = PLondon=Greek Papyri in the British Museum—List 4

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

Hdt. Hdt. = Herodotus, V b.c.—List 5

  1. X. = Xenophon, V–IV b.c.—List 5

MPol MPol = Martyrdom of Polycarp; after II a.d.—List 1

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 717
